item.title: Development-as-content pipeline — every commit generates discoverable content
item.intent: ClawHQ's development work must produce discoverable content as a byproduct. One-time launch events decay in 72 hours. Compounding content solves the discovery problem without a separate content calendar.
item.description: The evolved strategy identified discovery decay as a critical wound. The fix: every development action produces content. Bug fix → postmortem article. New blueprint → tutorial walkthrough. OpenClaw breaking change → 'what broke and how we fixed it.' This is a process change, not a feature. Implementation: (1) CHANGELOG entries written as narratives, not version lists, (2) template for converting commits/PRs into technical articles, (3) docs/articles/ directory for long-form content derived from development, (4) PROBLEMS.md already exists and is evergreen SEO — formalize the update process.
steps: Create docs/articles/ directory for technical content
Create article template: title, problem, context, solution, what we learned, ClawHQ relevance
Rewrite CHANGELOG.md entries as narratives (not just version bumps)
Write first article from existing work: '14 Ways Your OpenClaw Agent Silently Breaks' (from PROBLEMS.md content)
Write second article from BUG-125: 'Why Regex YAML Patching Will Corrupt Your Docker Config'
Add CONTRIBUTING.md section on development-as-content expectations
Document the process: each PR that fixes a landmine or adds a blueprint should include a content draft
acceptanceCriteria: docs/articles/ directory exists with article template
At least 2 articles written from existing development work
CHANGELOG.md entries are narrative, not version lists
Process documented in CONTRIBUTING.md for future development
